title: Johnson & Johnson — Hematology — Thesis
html:
J&J Hematology Thesis Tracker — Vinoth's POV

TA: Multiple Myeloma (DARZALEX, TECVAYLI, TALVEY, CARVYKTI) Initiated: 2026-04-24 Last reviewed: 2026-04-24 Review cadence: Quarterly (next: July 2026)

Thesis 1: TEC-DARA is the 2L inflection that reshapes the treatment sequence

The bet: The TECVAYLI + DARZALEX FASPRO combo (FDA approved Q1 2026) will become the standard of care in 2L RRMM within 12 months, displacing Kyprolis-based and DARA-triplet regimens. This moves bispecifics from "3L+ last resort" to "2L default," fundamentally reshaping the multiple myeloma treatment algorithm.

Why this matters for Vinoth: Every ZoomRx study in hematology — KPI tracker, ATU, MSL effectiveness — will now track adoption of a therapy moving EARLIER in the treatment sequence. The measurement framework needs to pivot from "late-line market share" to "2L new starts." This changes what J&J asks us to track.

Evidence FOR:

  • MajesTEC-3 Phase III: TEC-DARA reduced risk of progression/death by 83% in 2L RRMM
  • MajesTEC-9: TECVAYLI monotherapy reduces risk as early as first relapse
  • ZoomRx KPI tracker (Apr 2026): 52% of oncologists view TEC-DARA as appropriate for 2L patients
  • Physicians intending to prescribe TEC-DARA in 2L expect to shift away from Kyprolis-based regimens (40%) and DARA-triplets (31%)
  • TECVAYLI sequential growth of 14.2% in Q1 2026, attributed to community expansion + TEC-DARA approval

Evidence AGAINST:

  • CARVYKTI (CAR-T) still edged TEC-DARA in 2L physician preference: 57% vs 52%
  • CAR-T offers one-time treatment vs indefinite bispecific therapy — some physicians and patients prefer the "one and done" model
  • TEC-DARA requires step-up dosing and hospitalization, limiting community adoption speed
  • Infection risk from continuous bispecific therapy (hypogammaglobulinemia) is a growing concern among community oncologists

Confidence: High. The data is strong and the FDA approval is in hand. Adoption pace is the variable.

What to watch: Q2 2026 KPI tracker — does 2L new start share for TEC-DARA accelerate post-approval? CARVYKTI 2L data (CARTITUDE-6) could change the dynamic.

Thesis 2: DARZALEX is the annuity that funds the franchise — and it's not done growing

The bet: DARZALEX will surpass $16B in FY2026 revenue, driven by continued frontline share capture. The ~12-point frontline share gain isn't plateauing — it's still mid-cycle because the SubQ convenience advantage hasn't fully penetrated community practices. DARZALEX is the financial engine that gives J&J the runway to invest in bispecific and CAR-T market-shaping.

Why this matters for Vinoth: DARZALEX's dominance in frontline means ZoomRx's tracking should differentiate between "DARZALEX as backbone" (where the drug is used as part of the regimen) and "DARZALEX market dynamics" (competitive pressure from biosimilars, isatuximab). The BD opportunity is in new studies that track DARZALEX in novel combinations (e.g., with bispecifics in earlier lines).

Evidence FOR:

  • $14.4B FY2025, +21-24% growth across quarters
  • Frontline share gains of ~12 points — the strongest growth vector in the entire J&J portfolio
  • DARZALEX FASPRO (SubQ) has largely replaced IV in clinical practice
  • PERSEUS trial driving transplant-eligible frontline adoption
  • No meaningful anti-CD38 competition — Sarclisa (isatuximab) remains a distant second

Evidence AGAINST:

  • Daratumumab biosimilars will eventually arrive (timeline uncertain but 2028-2030 range)
  • Growth rate decelerating: +24.1% Q4 → +17.8% Q1 (still strong but base effect is real)
  • At $14.4B, the absolute dollar growth required to maintain percentage growth is enormous

Confidence: Very high on the trajectory through 2026. Medium confidence on sustaining growth beyond 2027 (biosimilar risk).

What to watch: FY2026 quarterly trajectory. Biosimilar litigation timelines. New frontline combination data (DARZALEX + bispecifics in NDMM studies).

Thesis 3: The bispecific vs CAR-T sequencing debate is the defining strategic question

The bet: There is no consensus on whether to use bispecifics (TECVAYLI, Elrexfio) before or after CAR-T (CARVYKTI, Abecma). This ambiguity will persist for 2-3 more years because no head-to-head data exists. Whoever frames the narrative — "bispecific first as bridge" vs "CAR-T first for deepest response" — wins the prescribing debate. J&J has the unique advantage of owning BOTH a bispecific (TECVAYLI) and a CAR-T (CARVYKTI), but they're competing against each other for the same patients.

Why this matters for Vinoth: This internal tension is a consulting gold mine. J&J needs data to optimize sequencing WITHIN their own portfolio, not just against competitors. ZoomRx can propose studies that track physician decision-making between J&J's own products (TECVAYLI vs CARVYKTI in 2L-3L) — a study no competitor can replicate.

Evidence FOR:

  • KPI tracker shows CARVYKTI and TEC-DARA nearly tied in 2L preference (57% vs 52%)
  • CAR-T manufacturing bottleneck (4-6 week wait) creates a natural "bridge with bispecific" use case
  • Oncologists are segmenting by patient profile: CARVYKTI for <75, fit; TEC-DARA for older, high-risk, CD38-sensitive
  • The Heme MSL study is specifically designed to track discussions across NDMM (DARZALEX), RRMM (bispecifics), AND CAR-T — suggesting J&J recognizes the sequencing question

Evidence AGAINST:

  • CARVYKTI is growing faster (+57.4%) and at $600M/quarter — if one product clearly wins, the debate resolves itself
  • CARTITUDE-6 (CARVYKTI in 2L) could make the case for CAR-T first, weakening the bridge argument
  • Some oncologists avoid bispecifics before CAR-T due to T-cell exhaustion concerns

Confidence: High that the debate persists through 2026. Medium confidence that J&J resolves the internal sequencing strategy (they may leave it to physician judgment).

What to watch: CARTITUDE-6 data. TEC-DARA 2L real-world adoption patterns. MSL tracking study results — do J&J MSLs have a consistent message on sequencing?

Thesis 4: Community hematology adoption is the volume unlock — and bispecifics are cracking it

The bet: Unlike lung cancer (where community was a barrier for Rybrevant's IV administration), hematology bispecifics are successfully expanding into community settings. TECVAYLI community growth was specifically called out in Q1 2026 earnings. If this continues, the total addressable prescriber pool expands from ~2,000 academic hematologists to ~8,000+ community oncologists who see MM patients.

Why this matters for Vinoth: Community expansion changes who ZoomRx should be sampling. The current studies are likely over-indexed to academic centers. As bispecifics move into community, the measurement framework needs to capture community oncologist behavior — which is more guideline-driven, more risk-averse, and more influenced by safety/convenience than academic physicians.

Evidence FOR:

  • J&J earnings Q1 2026: TECVAYLI community expansion specifically cited as growth driver
  • TALVEY also growing via community expansion (+72.8%)
  • DARZALEX FASPRO (SubQ) paved the way — community practices already have anti-CD38 experience
  • Step-up dosing protocols are becoming more standardized, lowering the activation barrier

Evidence AGAINST:

  • Bispecific CRS management still requires infusion center infrastructure and monitoring
  • Community oncologists see fewer MM patients (lower volume = less experience with CRS management)
  • Infection monitoring and IVIG replacement for hypogammaglobulinemia add complexity
  • CAR-T remains academic-only — community oncologists may be less comfortable with the bispecific-CAR-T sequencing decision

Confidence: Medium-high. The signal from earnings is real, but the magnitude of community penetration needs quantification from ZoomRx data.

What to watch: ZoomRx KPI tracker segmented by practice setting. MSL tracking study community vs academic split. TALVEY community adoption rate as a leading indicator.

Thesis 5: TALVEY (GPRC5D) is the portfolio insurance — and it's underappreciated

The bet: TALVEY's non-BCMA mechanism (GPRC5D target) makes it the only J&J product that works after BCMA-directed therapy failure (post-TECVAYLI, post-CARVYKTI, post-Elrexfio). As more patients cycle through BCMA therapies, TALVEY becomes the last line of defense. It's growing fastest (+72.8%) but getting the least management attention on earnings calls. This underappreciation is a positioning opportunity.

Why this matters for Vinoth: ZoomRx should be tracking TALVEY awareness and prescribing intent separately from BCMA bispecifics. If TALVEY is being used as a "rescue after BCMA failure" rather than a competing option, the measurement framework needs to capture this sequential positioning — not just head-to-head share.

Evidence FOR:

  • Fastest growth in the portfolio: +60.8% Q3, +72.8% Q1 2026
  • Unique GPRC5D target — no direct competitors on the same target
  • KPI tracker shows TALVEY viewed as appropriate for 76% of 4L+ patients and 54% of CD38-refractory patients
  • Post-BCMA sequencing is a growing clinical need as more patients receive TECVAYLI/CARVYKTI/Elrexfio in earlier lines

Evidence AGAINST:

  • Revenue still small (~$122M+ per quarter) compared to DARZALEX ($4B) or CARVYKTI ($600M)
  • Skin toxicity (GPRC5D-related: taste changes, nail changes, skin peeling) is a distinctive AE profile that some physicians find challenging
  • No Phase III data in earlier lines — currently a late-line option only
  • Could be disrupted by next-gen BCMA approaches that overcome resistance

Confidence: Medium. The mechanism and positioning are sound, but the size of the "post-BCMA rescue" market depends on how many patients fail BCMA-directed therapies — which itself depends on how effective TEC-DARA and CARVYKTI are in earlier lines.

What to watch: TALVEY earlier-line studies (if any initiated). Post-BCMA prescribing patterns in the KPI tracker. AE management data and its impact on community adoption.

Quarterly Review Log

Date Key changes Trigger
2026-04-24 Initial thesis set created J&J Hematology system build

Back to TA Knowledge Hub | Home
